text:
# Nova System Log

## 2026-05-18
- SSH access working
- Base folder structure created
- Beginning long-term Nova setup

# Nova Architecture

## Overview

Nova is Tyler's local AI assistant and automation ecosystem.

Primary goals:
- Long-term project memory
- Local AI assistance
- Automation and scheduling
- Home Assistant integration
- Voice interaction
- System monitoring
- Robotics support
- Permanent and portable architecture

---

# Core Philosophy

Nova's permanent core consists of:
- scripts
- memory
- configuration
- documentation
- Git version history

External services are modular and replaceable.

Nova should:
- remain portable
- avoid vendor lock-in
- survive hardware replacement
- eventually self-monitor and partially self-repair

---

# Current Hardware

## Nova Server
- HP Pro 3515 Series
- Linux
- 16GB DDR3 RAM
- AMD Radeon HD 7540D
- Docker installed
- Tailscale installed

## Gaming PC
Purpose:
- Primary workstation
- VS Code remote development
- Future speaker/audio output

- Future voice interaction
- Future GPU acceleration

## Laptop
Purpose:
- Portable Nova administration
- Remote VS Code editing
- Diagnostics and field development

## Raspberry Pi Systems
Planned purposes:
- RTK GPS
- Robotics
- Sensors
- Mower systems
- Distributed automation nodes

---

# Current Software Stack

## AI
- Ollama
- Open WebUI
- llama3.2:3b
- qwen2.5-coder planned

## Automation
- Home Assistant
- Docker containers

## Development
- VS Code Remote SSH
- Git
- GitHub planned

## Networking
- Tailscale
- SSH remote access

---

# Current Nova Features

- Local AI chat interface
- Morning briefing script
- Weather reporting
- Dockerized services
- Git version control
- Structured memory folders
- Remote editing from multiple machines

---

# Folder Structure

```text
Nova/
├── memory/
├── scripts/
├── logs/
├── docs/
├── backups/
├── tools/
├── configs/
└── agents/

# Nova Development Log - 2026-05-19

## Major Progress

Tonight Nova transitioned from a basic AI install into a structured local AI ecosystem.

Completed:
- Installed Ollama
- Installed Open WebUI
- Installed Docker
- Configured remote VS Code workflow
- Added Home Assistant
- Increased Nova server RAM from 8GB to 16GB
- Created Git repository for Nova
- Created structured memory folders
- Added morning briefing automation
- Added weather integration
- Added scheduled cron task
- Connected gaming PC and laptop to Nova through VS Code Remote SSH

---

# Current Working Features

## AI
- Local LLM support working
- llama3.2:3b installed
- Open WebUI accessible through browser

## Automation
- Morning briefing script functional
- Weather pulling from wttr.in
- Daily scheduled task operational

## Development
- Git initialized
- VS Code remote editing working

- Multi-device editing confirmed

## Home Assistant
- Installed and operational
- HACS installed
- Tapo integration troubleshooting in progress

---

# Important Notes

## Architecture Direction
Nova should remain:
- modular
- portable
- text-based
- self-documented
- Git tracked

Core memory and scripts should survive replacement of external services.

---

# Future Priorities

## Immediate
- Speaker setup
- Voice output
- Better AI models
- Tapo smart bulb integration
- Searchable memory

## Medium Term
- Wake-word system
- Voice interaction
- System monitoring
- Automatic backups
- Self-repair scripts

## Long Term
- Autonomous robotics support
- Distributed AI system
- Workshop automation
- Local RAG memory system
- Self-healing infrastructure

---

# Lessons Learned

- Docker host networking solved Open WebUI connectivity issues
- VS Code Remote SSH greatly improves workflow

- Markdown + Git is a strong foundation for long-term AI memory
- Existing tools should be treated as replaceable modules
- Nova core should remain independent of vendor ecosystems

---

# Current System Status

Nova server operational.
Remote access operational.
AI models operational.
Morning automation operational.
Memory framework operational.

Nova foundation successfully established.
